ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Bond Or Waivers Of Bond
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Equitable, Non Pro-Rata Division Of Real Property And Approval Of Distribution Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-06-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deb Fox, via ZOOM, for Bennie Ferma, Administrator.

The court begins by noting the submissions requested at the previous calling of this matter and notes that the terms of the loan appear to be unfavorable to the borrower. Ms. Fox confirms that the terms of the loan call for a three-month interest only payment period after which the borrower intends to refinance. The court then queries Ms. Fox regarding how fees and expenses will be paid. Ms. Fox advises that the Administrator will be paying expenses out-of-pocket. The court then advises Ms. Fox that it will be ordering that bond be increased by $370,000. Ms. Fox reports that there is a hearing set Nov-01-2021 on an accounting petition and asks that the court consider waivers of bond, if she can obtain them.

The Petition for Equitable, Non Pro Rata Division, etc., is submitted. A Status Hearing on Increase in Bond is set for Nov-01-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Andam. Not reported.

Petition For Equitable, Non Pro-Rata Division Of Real Property And Approval Of Distribution Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-07-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deborah Fox, via ZOOM, for Bennie Ferma, Administrator.

The court begins by advising counsel that probate code 11950 is inapplicable because the beneficiaries have come to an agreement with regard to distribution. Ms. Fox provides oral argument in support of the petition and framing it under probate code 11950 including that the will does not include language regarding pro rata distribution. The court instructs Ms. Fox to file a supplemental declaration to support her position with regard to the code. Ms. Fox also reports that all beneficiaries have approved the loan and the Administrator will be responsible for securing the loan. The court then queries counsel as to whether the filing of a supplement including terms of the agreement and the filing of an amended petition would require new notice. Ms. Fox reports that she believes that she can obtain waivers of notice. The court instructs Ms. Fox to file an amended petition including the terms of proposed loan by the next calling of this matter. Ms. Fox states that she has encountered issues with her filings being delayed due to the short staffing issues that the court is currently experiencing. Ms. Fox is advised to contact the assigned Examiner to discuss options including possibly submitting a courtesy copy of the petition before it has been reviewed by the filing clerks.

The Petition for Equitable, Non-Pro Rata Distribution, etc., is continued to 9/28/2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.?

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
